definition: The lawful conditionality by which, when this is, that comes to be.
notes: OSF editorial authority now treats Buddhadasa Bhikkhu as primary for this term. Conditionality remains the default because it is the cleanest reusable English rendering, but the repository now treats idappaccayat? as a core OSF doctrinal key rather than a minor supporting technicality.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose focused on the morphology of the term
context_rules.notes: Use when the idiom of this conditioned by this is central.

context_rules.context: In Buddhadasa-style explanation of natural law
context_rules.notes: Notes may explicitly describe it as the natural law behind dependent arising.

context_rules.context: In Buddhadasa-line explanation of the natural law behind dependent arising
context_rules.notes: Primary-source-backed default within the OSF line.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose focused on the literal structure of this conditioned by this
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when the morphology matters.

example_phrases.translation: when this is, this comes to be
